summary: WIN-053.c5 H3 closed via single find_replace patch on data/wins.json:WIN-053.detail_evidence inserting a new ~270-word paragraph addressing the surface-aperture re-interpretation defender pivot (distinct from the narrow-channel-return and Maxwellian-vs-aetheric rescues already foreclosed). Two structural rebuttals: (i) symmetric flux conservation requires A=C exactly while fitted values differ ~4% (62,376 vs 64,852 nT) and that 4% tracks observed WMM2025/IGRF-13 pole asymmetry — coefficients are curve-fit to observation or implicitly borrowing geodynamo asymmetry; (ii) both coefficients carry positive signs (both apertures as flux sources) — directional sign contradiction documented earlier persists unchanged. Kernel-of-truth (closed two-aperture circuit demands similar fields at both ends) acknowledged head-on. Anchor uniqueness verified count=1. No new external citations required (WMM2025/IGRF-13 NCEI URLs already used in WIN-053.detail_evidence). After patch applied: close ISS-1243 as fixed-pending-verification. Update tracker EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1243 status → integrated.
